scoring.primary_reward: highest score wins the investigative lead
scoring.secondary_reward: score contributes to an end-of-game award later

player_count_behavior.4: full simultaneous individual boards with leaderboard
player_count_behavior.5: same as four players, slightly tighter leaderboard spread
player_count_behavior.6: same as four players, with higher pressure on the top rank
player_count_behavior.7: same as four players, but shorter visible scoring windows
player_count_behavior.8: same as four players, but shorter visible scoring windows

authored_content.package_summary: A Nightcap-safe match-3 clue run that awards the best score with the primary investigative lead.

surface_contract.phone: private board, timer, personal score, combo feedback, and result
surface_contract.shared_display: leaderboard, narrator commentary, and session-safe status
surface_contract.host: controls, progress, tie handling, and fallback state

tile_language.motive: a pressure point in the room
tile_language.alibi: a claim that must hold up
tile_language.witness: a person or source that saw something

surface_rules.shared_display: never reveal private board state or private clues
surface_rules.phone: may show only the local player's board and result state
surface_rules.host: may show scoring, fallback, and tie resolution state

safety: no private clue leakage to the shared display

behavioral_outputs.description: Final score earned by the participant.
Number of swaps attempted by the participant.
Number of combo chains achieved by the participant.
Longest cascade chain reached during the run.
Number of attempted swaps that produced no match.
Final leaderboard position for the participant.
Score difference between the top two leaderboard positions.
Whether the run ended by timeout.
Whether the reduced fallback clue was required.
Count of special-tile activations achieved by the participant.
